understanding.goal_understanding: The goal in each level was to place every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with `+` in the semantic grid, with the requirement that if multiple `+` tiles exist they must all be occupied simultaneously to trigger WIN; completing all levels wins the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (`MOVE_*`) attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per move in the chosen direction, but movement could fail when blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects. Some adjacent objects could be pushable, allowing the selected piece to move while pushing the object in the same direction if space permitted. Walls (`#`) generally acted as impassable obstacles (though their exact blocking behavior could vary by level). When multiple selectables existed, `CLICK` on a selectable’s pixel position switched control to that piece; the `movement.<dir>_blocked` flags were only hints about adjacency and did not guarantee success or failure.
